extracted.brandBackground: Gorilla Property Services operates a commercial and residential property maintenance service business.

leadership.name: Andrew Edwards
leadership.background: President of Gorilla Franchising USA, Inc. and Gorilla Franchise Services Ltd. since March 2021.
leadership.whyItMatters: He is the primary executive leader and owner of the brand.

cohorts.label: Canadian Franchisees Gross Revenue Table 1

cohorts.basis: 2 multi-unit franchisees owning two territories each.

cohorts.basis: Historical representation based on data for 6 Canadian franchisees who provided complete information.

item19.notes: All dollar amounts in Item 19 are presented in CAD and not USD.

lineItems.notes: Non-refundable fee for the first territory.

lineItems.category: Initial training fees, travel, lodging, food, and other expenses while training

lineItems.notes: Estimated cost for ladder and compressor.
Purchase or lease of compatible hardware.
Legal, entity organization, and accounting fees.
Monthly premium costs for required insurances.
Includes pre-opening, grand opening, and first 3 months.
Estimated operating expenses for the first 3 months.

hiddenCosts.name: Minimum Royalty Fee
hiddenCosts.description: A minimum monthly royalty of $500 applies after 6 months of opening.

hiddenCosts.name: Minimum National Advertising Fund Fee
hiddenCosts.description: The National Advertising Fund Fee is the greater of $429 plus taxes or 2% of monthly gross sales.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation only in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

operationalRisks.title: Short Operating History
operationalRisks.description: The franchisor is at an early stage of development and has a limited operating history in the US.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: You must make minimum royalty or advertising fund payments regardless of sales levels.

operationalRisks.title: Minimum Sales Performance Required
operationalRisks.description: You must maintain minimum sales performance levels or risk losing territorial rights or termination.

extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise offers commercial and residential property maintenance services, which falls under home and trade services.
extracted.staffingRationale: The business is typically operated from a home office with mobile service vans, requiring a small crew.

riskReasons: 2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.

midCohort.label: Canadian Franchisees Profit and Loss Table 4 Median

source.label: Canadian Franchisees Profit and Loss Table 4 Median

notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $171,725 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $10,069/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise offers commercial and residential property maintenance services, which falls under home and trade services.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 0.3–0.7 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 2–6% category band.
Estimated at the 25–40% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 10–21% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
